programs.name: Yoga
programs.description: Relax your body and mind with yoga.  Learn and practice yoga poses and postures to improve your balance, breathing, flexibility and strength.

programs.name: Yoga: Chair
programs.description: Gain all the benefits of yoga with the added support of a chair or your walker. Combining gentle stretches and traditional poses, you will improve your flexibility, strength, breathing and relaxation with every movement.

programs.name: Yoga: Chair
programs.description: Gain all the benefits of yoga with the added support of a chair or your walker. Combining gentle stretches and traditional poses, you will improve your flexibility, strength, breathing and relaxation with every movement.

programs.name: Ball Hockey: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports activities are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In this non-ice version of hockey played with a ball, you will learn basics like passing, shooting and stick handling as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.Equipment: Players must bring a CSA-approved helmet and hockey gloves.

programs.name: Cooking: Baking
programs.description: Whether you are making breads, desserts or pastries; something savoury or sweet, this activity will help you reach new culinary delights. Come learn the basics of cooking and how to prepare delicious and nutritious recipes inspired by Canada’s Food Guide. From measuring and mixing ingredients to food safety, you will gain confidence in the kitchen.The City of Toronto and its staff cannot guarantee that the foods or supplies within its cooking programs are free of allergens. Notify staff at your location of any food allergies or dietary restrictions you may have.

programs.name: Dance: Ballet
programs.description: This classic style of dance is all about graceful movements. Learn basic ballet positions, postures and proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Bollywood
programs.description: This fusion of Bhangra, jazz, hip hop and modern dance is known to be energetic and expressive. Learn traditional Indian dance steps, footwork and hand movements through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Drama
programs.description: Here's an early introduction to the stage and performance art. Through dramatic play and skits learn to express emotion with your body, face and voice, to improve your performing arts skills.

programs.name: Drama
programs.description: Here's an early introduction to the stage and performance art. Through dramatic play and skits learn to express emotion with your body, face and voice, to improve your performing arts skills.

programs.name: Drawing
programs.description: Bring your imagination to life on paper. Learn the basics of how to draw using shapes and colours to create pictures with markers, crayons and pencil crayons.

programs.name: Drawing
programs.description: Bring your imagination to life on paper. Learn the basics of how to draw using shapes and colours to create pictures with markers, crayons and pencil crayons.

programs.name: Drawing and Painting
programs.description: An introductory class where participants will learn the basics of drawing and painting. Explore your creativity through pencils, pastels, acrylic paint, and watercolour paint.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 1
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively, and float with assistance. One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 2
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Develop front and back floats and staying afloat in the water. Learn front and back glide and going underwater. One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 1.

programs.name: Gymnastics
programs.description: Flexibility, strength, coordination and balance, gymnastics has it all. With a focus on fun and safety, you will learn and practice basic skills such as walks, jumps, rolls and turns on various apparatuses. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Gymnastics
programs.description: Flexibility, strength, coordination and balance, gymnastics has it all. With a focus on fun and safety, you will learn and practice basic skills such as walks, jumps, rolls and turns on various apparatuses.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun activities and games, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Prerequisite: Able to go on the ice alone and stand and walk on skates off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate with Caregiver
programs.description: It's never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun games and activities, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping. A caregiver who is at least 14 years of age and a competent skater must actively participate in this activity. Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage (for the child) and a CSA-approved hockey helmet (for the caregiver). All skaters require hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Making Music with Caregiver
programs.description: Guitar is needed, as you clap, sing, laugh and play and will discover making music is fun. Through a variety of activities, crafts and games you will learn the basics of rhythm and sounds. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Martial Arts - Beginner
programs.description: This activity introduces you to martial arts.  Here you will develop fundamental martial art skills, simple sparring skills, tactical concepts, show willingness to engage in a variety of sport activities and have confidence to try new skills.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 3
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop staying afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Learn front crawl, diving and back swim. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 meters. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 2 or Preschool Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 3
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop staying afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Learn front crawl, diving and back swim. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 meters. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 2 or Preschool Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 4
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl and treading water. Learn side breathing, somersault, whip-kick and forward roll. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 metres. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 3.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 4
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl and treading water. Learn side breathing, somersault, whip-kick and forward roll. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 metres. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 3.
programs.location: Gus Ryder Pool And Health Club

programs.name: Ultra Swim 4 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl and treading water. Learn side breathing, somersault, whip-kick and forward roll.  Work towards a continuous swim of 25 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 3.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 6
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and eggbeater kick. Learn stride entries, surface dives, breaststroke and interval training. Work towards a continuous swim of 150 metres. This activity is for all girls.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 5.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 7
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives, eggbeater kick, front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and breaststroke. Learn shallow dives into deep water and treading water while fully clothed. Work towards a continuous swim of 200 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 6.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 8
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives with underwater swimming, interval training, object support and all strokes. Work towards a continuous swim of 300 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 7.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 8 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives with underwater swimming, interval training, object support and all strokes. Work towards a continuous swim of 300 metres. Class size is limited to four participants. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 7.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 9
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop non-contact rescues and stroke improvement. Learn stride jump. Work towards a continuous swim of 400 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 8.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 9 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop non-contact rescues and stroke improvement. Learn stride jump. Work towards a continuous swim of 400 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 8.
programs.location: Gus Ryder Pool And Health Club

programs.name: Dance: Hip Hop
programs.description: Hip Hop is a street dance that uses strong, fast and fluid movements. Learn basic steps like body rolls, contractions, bounces and waves through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 3
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop staying afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Learn front crawl, diving and back swim. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 meters. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 2 or Preschool Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 3
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop staying afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Learn front crawl, diving and back swim. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 meters. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 2 or Preschool Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 4
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl and treading water. Learn side breathing, somersault, whip-kick and forward roll. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 metres. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 3.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 4
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl and treading water. Learn side breathing, somersault, whip-kick and forward roll. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 metres. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 3.
programs.location: Gus Ryder Pool And Health Club

programs.name: Ultra Swim 4 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl and treading water. Learn side breathing, somersault, whip-kick and forward roll.  Work towards a continuous swim of 25 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 3.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 6
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and eggbeater kick. Learn stride entries, surface dives, breaststroke and interval training. Work towards a continuous swim of 150 metres. This activity is for all girls.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 5.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 7
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives, eggbeater kick, front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and breaststroke. Learn shallow dives into deep water and treading water while fully clothed. Work towards a continuous swim of 200 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 6.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 8
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives with underwater swimming, interval training, object support and all strokes. Work towards a continuous swim of 300 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 7.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 8 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives with underwater swimming, interval training, object support and all strokes. Work towards a continuous swim of 300 metres. Class size is limited to four participants. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 7.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 9
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop non-contact rescues and stroke improvement. Learn stride jump. Work towards a continuous swim of 400 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 8.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 9 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop non-contact rescues and stroke improvement. Learn stride jump. Work towards a continuous swim of 400 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 8.

programs.description: Hip Hop is a street dance that uses strong, fast and fluid movements. Learn basic steps like body rolls, contractions, bounces and waves through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 1
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively, and float with assistance. One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 2
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Develop front and back floats and staying afloat in the water. Learn front and back glide and going underwater. One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 1.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 2
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Develop front and back floats and staying afloat in the water. Learn front and back glide and going underwater. One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 1.

programs.name: Karate - Beginner
programs.description: This activity introduces you to martial arts.  Here you will develop fundamental martial art skills, simple sparring skills, tactical concepts, show willingness to engage in a variety of sport activities and have confidence to try new skills.

programs.name: Martial Arts - Beginner with Caregiver
programs.description: Learn to move in this martial arts program where you will learn fundamental martial art skills, build confidence, learn etiquette and rules and learn to respect the sport, the instructor and other participants.

programs.name: Music: Keyboard
programs.description: Fun and fundamentals shape this introduction to keyboarding. Here you will learn proper hand positions, basic scales and how to read notes. As you continue to practice and learn how to read music you will work towards performing simple pieces.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 6
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and eggbeater kick. programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives, eggbeater kick, front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and breaststroke. Learn shallow dives into deep water and treading water while fully clothed. Work towards a continuous swim of 200 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 6.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 8
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives with underwater swimming, interval training, object support and all strokes. Work towards a continuous swim of 300 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 7.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 8 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives with underwater swimming, interval training, object support and all strokes. programs.name: Ultra Swim 9
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop non-contact rescues and stroke improvement. Learn stride jump. Work towards a continuous swim of 400 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 8.

programs.name: Dance: Ballet
programs.description: This classic style of dance is all about graceful movements. Learn basic ballet positions, postures and proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Hip Hop
programs.description: Hip Hop is a street dance that uses strong, fast and fluid movements. Learn basic steps like body rolls, contractions, bounces and waves through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Hip Hop
programs.description: Hip Hop is a street dance that uses strong, fast and fluid movements. Learn basic steps like body rolls, contractions, bounces and waves through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 1
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively, and float with assistance. One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 2
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Develop front and back floats and staying afloat in the water. Learn front and back glide and going underwater. One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 1.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 2
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Develop front and back floats and staying afloat in the water. Learn front and back glide and going underwater. One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 1.

programs.name: Karate - Beginner
programs.description: This activity introduces you to martial arts.  Here you will develop fundamental martial art skills, simple sparring skills, tactical concepts, show willingness to engage in a variety of sport activities and have confidence to try new skills.

programs.name: Martial Arts - Beginner with Caregiver
programs.description: Learn to move in this martial arts program where you will learn fundamental martial art skills, build confidence, learn etiquette and rules and learn to respect the sport, the instructor and other participants.

programs.name: Music: Keyboard
programs.description: Fun and fundamentals shape this introduction to keyboarding. Here you will learn proper hand positions, basic scales and how to read notes. As you continue to practice and learn how to read music you will work towards performing simple pieces.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

Work towards a continuous swim of 10 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 2 or Preschool Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 4
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl and treading water. Learn side breathing, somersault, whip-kick and forward roll. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 metres. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 3.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 4
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl and treading water. Learn side breathing, somersault, whip-kick and forward roll. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 metres. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 3.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Dance: Hip Hop with Recital
programs.description: Hip Hop is a street dance that uses strong, fast and fluid movements. Learn basic steps like body rolls, contractions, bounces and waves through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Ballet with Recital
programs.description: This classic style of dance is all about graceful movements. Learn basic ballet positions, postures and proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Drama
programs.description: Here's an early introduction to the stage and performance art. Through dramatic play and skits learn to express emotion with your body, face and voice, to improve your performing arts skills.

programs.name: Dance: Tap with Recital
programs.description: Learn the fundamentals of tap dancing such as shuffle, roll, ball tap and brush to help develop and demonstrate a tap routine in a friendly and enjoyable environment.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 1
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Learn basics like balance and posture, falling and standing up and forward movement. As you gain confidence on the ice, you will work towards forward skating, gliding, stopping and backward skating. Prerequisite: Able to stand and walk on skates with straight ankles off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun activities and games, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Prerequisite: Able to go on the ice alone and stand and walk on skates off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate with Caregiver
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun games and activities, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping. A caregiver who is at least 14 years of age and a competent skater must actively participate in this activity.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage (for the child) and a CSA-approved hockey helmet (for the caregiver). All skaters require hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Gymnastics: Competitive with Showcase
programs.description: Are you ready for a challenge? Then consider this activity designed for those who seek to master a combination of gymnastic skills and create unique, signature routines. As you continue to practice and develop your skills, you will work towards competing in a City-wide gymnastics showcase. Contact your local activity location to arrange an assessment or for more details. Prerequisite: Gymnastics (Advanced).
programs.location: Joseph J. Piccininni Cmty Rec Centre
programs.time: 5:00 PM - 1:00 PM on the next day

programs.name: Gymnastics: Competitive with Showcase
programs.description: Flexibility, strength, coordination and balance, gymnastics has it all. With a focus on improving strength and form, you will learn and practice skills such as front and back rolls, handstands, cartwheels, bridges, leaps, jumps and turns on a variety of apparatuses. Prerequisite: Gymnastics (Competitive).

programs.name: Gymnastics: Competitive with Showcase
programs.description: Are you ready for a challenge? Then consider this activity designed for those who seek to master a combination of gymnastic skills and create unique, signature routines. As you continue to practice and develop your skills, you will work towards competing in a City-wide gymnastics showcase. Contact your local activity location to arrange an assessment or for more details. Prerequisite: Gymnastics (Advanced).

programs.name: Gymnastics: Competitive with Showcase
programs.description: Flexibility, strength, coordination and balance, gymnastics has it all. With a focus on improving strength and form, you will learn and practice skills such as front and back rolls, handstands, cartwheels, bridges, leaps, jumps and turns on a variety of apparatuses. Prerequisite: Gymnastics (Competitive).
programs.time: 5:30 PM - 3:00 PM on the next day

programs.name: Gymnastics: Competitive with Showcase

Prerequisite: Gymnastics (Competitive).

programs.description: This classic style of dance is all about graceful movements. Learn basic ballet positions, postures and proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Club: Explorers Walking and Social

programs.name: Music: Keyboard - Advanced
programs.description: In advanced music you will build on fun and fundamentals and work towards refinement. Here you will continue to practice proper hand positions and basic scales and learn more complex rhythms and the use of foot pedals. As you continue to practice you will work towards performing more complex pieces.

programs.name: Pickleball: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In pickleball, a fast-moving racquet sport that combines elements from tennis, badminton and table tennis, you will learn basics like serving, hitting and rallying as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Pottery: Wheel
programs.description: Create with clay on a pottery wheel. Learn the basics of how to use this tool including specific techniques such as slab, coil and pinch pot to create and finish 3D forms.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 1: Penguin
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively and float with assistance.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 2: Stingray
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop entering and exiting the pool, bobbing, and floating without assistance. Learn front swim. Work towards a continuous swim of three metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Preschool Swim 1 or Guardian Swim 3.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 2: Stingray
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop entering and exiting the pool, bobbing, and floating without assistance. Learn front swim. Work towards a continuous swim of three metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Preschool Swim 1 or Guardian Swim 3.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 3: Otter
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop front and back swim. Learn side glides, sculling and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of five metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 2.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 3: Otter
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop front and back swim. Learn side glides, sculling and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of five metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 2.

programs.name: Science
programs.description: Does science excite you? If so, come explore science through hands-on activities and conduct fun and exciting experiments.

programs.name: Science: Scientific Discovery
programs.description: Does science excite you? If so, come explore science through hands-on activities and conduct fun and exciting experiments.

programs.name: Sewing
programs.description: Come get an introduction to this handy skill. Learn how to read a pattern, cut material, stich by hand or use a sewing machine with the aim of completing a simple project.

programs.name: Soccer: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement skills such as agility, balance and coordination. In soccer you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading, as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Functional Fit
programs.description: This conditioning workout is designed to help you build strength to support day-to-day activities. At your own pace, you will practice basic movements like pushing, pulling, bending and squatting to improve your overall functional fitness.

programs.name: Games: Chess
programs.description: From active to interactive and simple to strategic, games are a great way to gain new skills. In chess, a two-player game where game pieces are used to move across the board with the object to checkmate your opponent, you will learn patience, tactics and critical thinking as well as game rules.

programs.name: Introduction to Machine Knitting: Bags and Home Decor
programs.description: Using our LK150 knitting machines and your own yarn, create your custom designs like bags, pillowcases and more! Students will learnthe fundamentals of machine knitting including skills like casting on and off, forming slip knots and stitches, and checking tension. Thisworkshop requires an additional $15 material fee (cash only), payable to instructor for practice materials. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner - Small Group
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun games and activities, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Able to go on the ice alone and stand and walk on skates off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Osteo Fit
programs.description: Help build and maintain stronger bones by increasing muscle strength with Osteo Fit. Suitable for individuals with low bone density or osteoporosis, exercises consist of slow-paced, weight-bearing movements with resistance.

programs.name: Painting: Acrylic
programs.description: Create pictures with acrylic paint. Learn how to mix and choose colours, how to use tools to create different textures and add layers of paint to create depth.

programs.name: Pickleball: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In pickleball, a fast-moving racquet sport that combines elements from tennis, badminton and table tennis, you will learn basics like serving, hitting and rallying as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 1: Penguin
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively and float with assistance.

programs.name: Recreation Discovery with Caregiver
programs.description: Embark on a recreational discovery with a focus on developing skills through creative, active and interactive activities. Programming will include storytelling, theme-based games, crafts, music and physical activity, while having fun with new friends. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Sewing
programs.description: Come get an introduction to this handy skill. Learn how to read a pattern, cut material, stich by hand or use a sewing machine with the aim of completing a simple project.

programs.name: Table Tennis: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills through play with recreational table tennis. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Tai Chi
programs.description: Embrace the body and mind through the practice of tai chi . Learn how to pair gentle movements that you will perform in a sequence with controlled breathing, to improve your flexibility, coordination, strength and mental concentration.

programs.name: Yoga
programs.description: Relax your body and mind with yoga.  Learn and practice yoga poses and postures to improve your balance, breathing, flexibility and strength.

programs.name: Yoga
programs.description: Relax your body and mind with yoga.  Learn and practice yoga poses and postures to improve your balance, breathing, flexibility and strength.

programs.name: Yoga: Chair
programs.description: Gain all the benefits of yoga with the added support of a chair or your walker. Combining gentle stretches and traditional poses, you will improve your flexibility, strength, breathing and relaxation with every movement.

programs.name: Yoga: Chair
programs.description: Gain all the benefits of yoga with the added support of a chair or your walker. Combining gentle stretches and traditional poses, you will improve your flexibility, strength, breathing and relaxation with every movement.

programs.name: Adult Swim 1
programs.description: Build confidence in the water one stroke at a time. Learn front and back swim, front, side and back glides, going underwater, breathing effectively and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 to 50 metres.

programs.name: Adult Swim 1
programs.description: Build confidence in the water one stroke at a time. Learn front and back swim, front, side and back glides, going underwater, breathing effectively and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 to 50 metres.

programs.name: Adult Swim 2
programs.description: Build confidence in deep water one stroke at a time. Develop front crawl, back crawl, glides and staying afloat in deep water. Learn stride entry, eggbeater kick and breaststroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 50 to 100 meters. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Adult Swim 1.

programs.name: Dance: Line Dance
programs.description: Are you ready to step up your dance skills with line dancing? Using basic steps to create repetitive combinations, you'll learn moves like the shuffle, grapevine, coaster step and mambo step through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.description: Hip Hop is a street dance that uses strong, fast and fluid movements. Learn basic steps like body rolls, contractions, bounces and waves through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 1
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Learn basics like balance and posture, falling and standing up and forward movement. As you gain confidence on the ice, you will work towards forward skating, gliding, stopping and backward skating. Prerequisite: Able to stand and walk on skates with straight ankles off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun activities and games, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Prerequisite: Able to go on the ice alone and stand and walk on skates off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate with Caregiver
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun games and activities, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping. A caregiver who is at least 14 years of age and a competent skater must actively participate in this activity.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage (for the child) and a CSA-approved hockey helmet (for the caregiver). All skaters require hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils

Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. programs.name: Ultra Swim 2 - Small Group
programs.description: Jump into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front swim, back kicks and glides. Learn how to stay afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Work towards a continuous swim of five metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 1 or Preschool Swim  3.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 3
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop staying afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Learn front crawl, diving and back swim. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 meters. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 2 or Preschool Swim 4.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 2 - Small Group
programs.description: Jump into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front swim, back kicks and glides. Learn how to stay afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Work towards a continuous swim of five metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 1 or Preschool Swim  3.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 2 - Small Group
programs.description: Jump into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front swim, back kicks and glides. Learn how to stay afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Work towards a continuous swim of five metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 1 or Preschool Swim  3.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 3
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop staying afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Learn front crawl, diving and back swim. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 meters. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 2 or Preschool Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 4
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl and treading water. Learn side breathing, somersault, whip-kick and forward roll. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 metres. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 3.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 5
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front dive, forward roll and front crawl. Learn back crawl, eggbeater kick and elementary backstroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 75 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 5 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front dive, forward roll and front crawl. Learn back crawl, eggbeater kick and elementary backstroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 75 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 6
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and eggbeater kick. Learn stride entries, surface dives, breaststroke and interval training.  Work towards a continuous swim of 150 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 5.
programs.location: Joseph J. Piccininni Cmty Rec Centre

programs.name: Ultra Swim 6 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and eggbeater kick. Learn stride entries, surface dives, breaststroke and interval training. Work towards a continuous swim of 150 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 5.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 7
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives, eggbeater kick, front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and breaststroke. Learn shallow dives into deep water and treading water while fully clothed. Work towards a continuous swim of 200 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 6.
programs.location: Joseph J. Piccininni Cmty Rec Centre

programs.name: Ultra Swim 8
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives with underwater swimming, interval training, object support and all strokes. Work towards a continuous swim of 300 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 7.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 9
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop non-contact rescues and stroke improvement. Learn stride jump. Work towards a continuous swim of 400 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 8.

programs.name: Art Club
programs.description: Experience working in a wide variety of art genres. Learn the basics of drawing, painting and sculpting, and with the help of both instruction and your imagination, make unique art projects.

programs.name: Ball Hockey: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports activities are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In this non-ice version of hockey played with a ball, you will learn basics like passing, shooting and stick handling as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.Equipment: Players must bring a CSA-approved helmet and hockey gloves.

programs.name: Basketball: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In basketball, you will learn basics like passing, dribbling, layups and shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Basketball: Instructional with Caregiver
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In basketball, you will learn basics like passing, dribbling, layups and shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play. A caregiver is required to actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Bronze Cross
programs.description: Bronze Cross begins the transition from lifesaving to lifeguarding. Candidates strengthen and expand their lifesaving skills and begin to apply the principles and techniques of active surveillance in aquatic facilities. Bronze Cross emphasizes the importance of teamwork and communication in preventing and responding to aquatic emergencies. Bronze Cross is a prerequisite for advanced training in the Society’s National Lifeguard and leadership certification programs. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 11 minutes or less by the course exam date. This course is generally offered in an extended nine-class format. Prerequisite: Bronze Medallion and Emergency First Aid with CPR-B (current or expired) or Standard First Aid with CPR-C  from an approved provider.Equipment: Bring your copy of the Canadian Lifesaving manual received in Bronze Medallion.

programs.name: Bronze Medallion and Emergency First Aid (CPR B)
programs.description: Bronze Medallion challenges the candidate both mentally and physically. Judgment, knowledge, skill and fitness – the four components of water rescue – form the basis of Bronze Medallion training. Candidates acquire the assessment and problem-solving skills needed to make good decisions in, on, and around the water. Bronze Medallion is a prerequisite for assistant lifeguard training in Bronze Cross. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 12 minutes or less by the course exam date. The Emergency First Aid (CPR-B) certification teaches victim assessment, CPR and addressing choking, as well as first aid treatment of external bleeding, heart attack, stroke and wound and burns. This course is generally offered in an extended nine-class format.Prerequisite: Minimum age is 13 years old or Bronze Star. Candidates with Bronze Star, that are under 13 years of age, can register for this course by calling 416-396-7378 or visiting an in-person registration site.

programs.name: Cooking
programs.description: Let’s get creative in the kitchen and have some fun. In this introduction to cooking, you will learn about healthy foods, the importance of safety and practice basic skills like mixing and measuring with easy-to-follow recipes that taste great.The City of Toronto and its staff cannot guarantee that the foods or supplies within its cooking programs are free of allergens. Notify staff at your location of any food allergies or dietary restrictions you may have.

programs.name: Cooking : Baking
programs.description: Let’s get creative in the kitchen and have some fun. In this introduction to cooking, you will learn about healthy foods, the importance of safety and practice basic skills like mixing and measuring with easy-to-follow recipes that taste great.The City of Toronto and its staff cannot guarantee that the foods or supplies within its cooking programs are free of allergens. Notify staff at your location of any food allergies or dietary restrictions you may have.

programs.name: Cooking: Creative Chefs
programs.description: Small cooks with big imaginations will have the chance to take over the kitchen. Starting with cooking basics, you will learn about healthy foods and the importance of kitchen safety, then through hands-on practice you will learn how to mix and measure, use cool kitchen tools and follow recipes that explore fun flavours that taste great.The City of Toronto and its staff cannot guarantee that the foods or supplies within its cooking programs are free of allergens. Notify staff at your location of any food allergies or dietary restrictions you may have.
programs.location: Joseph J. Piccininni Cmty Rec Centre

programs.name: Dance: Ballet
programs.description: This classic style of dance is all about graceful movements. Learn basic ballet positions, postures and proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography. A caregiver is required to actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Dance: Ballet
programs.description: This classic style of dance is all about graceful movements. Learn basic ballet positions, postures and proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography.* Introduce basic technique including barrework,body alignment and arm movements (port de bras)* basic barre exercises will build strength and correct placement* centre work will focus on simple movement steps,jumps and turns* participants will learn a short choreographed routine that will be performed on the last day

programs.name: Dance: Ballet with Caregiver
programs.description: This classic style of dance is all about graceful movements. Learn basic ballet positions, postures and proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography. A caregiver is required to actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Dance: Ballet with Caregiver
programs.description: This classic style of dance is all about graceful movements. Learn basic ballet positions, postures and proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography. A caregiver is required to actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Dance: Ballet
programs.description: This classic style of dance is all about graceful movements. Learn basic ballet positions, postures and proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography.* Introduce basic technique including barrework,body alignment and arm movements (port de bras)* basic barre exercises will build strength and correct placement* centre work will focus on simple movement steps,jumps and turns* participants will learn a short choreographed routine that will be performed on the last day

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 5
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front dive, forward roll and front crawl. Learn back crawl, eggbeater kick and elementary backstroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 75 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 5 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front dive, forward roll and front crawl. Learn back crawl, eggbeater kick and elementary backstroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 75 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 6
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and eggbeater kick. Learn stride entries, surface dives, breaststroke and interval training.  Work towards a continuous swim of 150 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 5.
programs.location: Joseph J. Piccininni Cmty Rec Centre

programs.name: Ultra Swim 6 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and eggbeater kick. Learn stride entries, surface dives, breaststroke and interval training. Work towards a continuous swim of 150 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 5.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 7
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives, eggbeater kick, front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and breaststroke. Learn shallow dives into deep water and treading water while fully clothed. Work towards a continuous swim of 200 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 6.
programs.location: Joseph J. Piccininni Cmty Rec Centre

programs.name: Ultra Swim 8
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives with underwater swimming, interval training, object support and all strokes. Work towards a continuous swim of 300 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 7.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 9
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop non-contact rescues and stroke improvement. Learn stride jump. Work towards a continuous swim of 400 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 8.

programs.name: Art Club
programs.description: Experience working in a wide variety of art genres. Learn the basics of drawing, painting and sculpting, and with the help of both instruction and your imagination, make unique art projects.

programs.name: Ball Hockey: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports activities are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In this non-ice version of hockey played with a ball, you will learn basics like passing, shooting and stick handling as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.Equipment: Players must bring a CSA-approved helmet and hockey gloves.

programs.name: Basketball: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In basketball, you will learn basics like passing, dribbling, layups and shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Basketball: Instructional with Caregiver
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In basketball, you will learn basics like passing, dribbling, layups and shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play. A caregiver is required to actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Bronze Cross
programs.description: Bronze Cross begins the transition from lifesaving to lifeguarding. Candidates strengthen and expand their lifesaving skills and begin to apply the principles and techniques of active surveillance in aquatic facilities. Bronze Cross emphasizes the importance of teamwork and communication in preventing and responding to aquatic emergencies. Bronze Cross is a prerequisite for advanced training in the Society’s National Lifeguard and leadership certification programs. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 11 minutes or less by the course exam date. This course is generally offered in an extended nine-class format. Prerequisite: Bronze Medallion and Emergency First Aid with CPR-B (current or expired) or Standard First Aid with CPR-C  from an approved provider.Equipment: Bring your copy of the Canadian Lifesaving manual received in Bronze Medallion.

programs.name: Bronze Medallion and Emergency First Aid (CPR B)
programs.description: Bronze Medallion challenges the candidate both mentally and physically. Judgment, knowledge, skill and fitness – the four components of water rescue – form the basis of Bronze Medallion training. Candidates acquire the assessment and problem-solving skills needed to make good decisions in, on, and around the water. Bronze Medallion is a prerequisite for assistant lifeguard training in Bronze Cross. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 12 minutes or less by the course exam date. The Emergency First Aid (CPR-B) certification teaches victim assessment, CPR and addressing choking, as well as first aid treatment of external bleeding, heart attack, stroke and wound and burns. This course is generally offered in an extended nine-class format.Prerequisite: Minimum age is 13 years old or Bronze Star. Candidates with Bronze Star, that are under 13 years of age, can register for this course by calling 416-396-7378 or visiting an in-person registration site.

Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.
